title: KOMSCO Synthetic Operations Knowledge Pack
heading: Issuance API service profile
text: Synthetic customer profile. The issuance-api workload runs in namespace komsco-prod and serves citizen-facing certificate issuance requests. It depends on oracle-svc for ledger reads, redis-cache for short-lived tokens, and audit-log-writer for immutable audit events. Normal symptoms during pressure are memory working set spikes, 5xx at the route, and delayed audit writes. Safe RCA starts with pod status, warning events, previous logs, memory metrics, route error rate, and dependency health.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Operations Knowledge Pack
heading: Settlement worker service profile
text: Synthetic customer profile. settlement-worker runs in komsco-batch during the nightly reconciliation window. It is CPU and memory heavy and may remain Pending when namespace quota, node allocatable memory, taints, or batch window concurrency are exhausted. Safe RCA checks FailedScheduling events, requests and limits, ResourceQuota usage, node pressure, and batch window policy before any scaling recommendation.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Operations Knowledge Pack
heading: Audit log writer service profile
text: Synthetic customer profile. audit-log-writer stores operational and transaction evidence for regulated workloads. If it is slow, issuance-api and settlement-worker may show queue growth or timeout logs. Safe RCA checks pod readiness, Kafka endpoint events, PVC attach state, and audit backlog metrics. Never expose raw audit payloads, user identifiers, or token values in CAS answers.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Operations Policy
heading: Read-only RCA boundary
text: CAS must stay read-only in v0.1.1. Allowed checks are get, list, watch, describe, previous logs, and read-only metric queries. Destructive or mutating actions such as delete, patch, rollout restart, scale, exec, and port-forward require a separate approved change workflow and must be written only as optional next steps after human approval.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Operations Policy
heading: Incident severity and escalation policy
text: Synthetic policy. SEV1 is customer-facing outage, data integrity risk, or security evidence loss. SEV2 is degraded service, repeated pod restart, route 5xx above threshold, or batch delay threatening the business window. CAS should recommend escalation when more than one critical dependency is affected, when audit-log-writer is degraded, or when read-only evidence is insufficient for a safe decision.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Operations Policy
heading: Change freeze and public-sector operations window
text: Synthetic policy. During monthly issuance peak, settlement close, or security audit windows, CAS must separate observation from remediation. It may suggest safe checks and console links, but any restart, image rollback, quota increase, or network policy change must be routed through the approved maintenance window.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic RCA Runbook
heading: issuance-api OOMKilled memory limit triage
text: When issuance-api is OOMKilled, compare last terminated reason, restart increase, container_memory_working_set_bytes, and kube_pod_container_resource_limits. A known synthetic pattern is report export or certificate bundle generation pushing working set above 768Mi. Safe checks are pod yaml, previous log tail, memory working set, memory limit, recent Warning events, and route 5xx correlation. Do not restart the pod during RCA.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Known Incident Library
heading: Known incident: report export memory surge
section_path_label: Known Incidents > issuance-api > report export OOM
text: Synthetic known incident. During high-volume certificate report export, issuance-api may allocate large PDF buffers and exceed the configured memory limit. Evidence usually includes previous logs containing report-export, Java heap, or allocation failure, memory working set near or above the limit, and restart increase within 30 minutes. Prevention is streaming export, limit baseline review, and workload-specific alerting.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic RCA Runbook
heading: CrashLoopBackOff caused by missing config or secret
text: For CrashLoopBackOff after deployment, inspect previous logs, waiting reason, exit code, mounted ConfigMap and Secret references, and recent image rollout. If previous logs mention missing environment variable, invalid profile, or failed secret mount, keep the recommendation read-only and ask the service owner to confirm the intended configuration before any patch.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic RCA Runbook
heading: settlement-worker Pending and FailedScheduling triage
text: When settlement-worker remains Pending, inspect FailedScheduling events, requested CPU and memory, namespace ResourceQuota, LimitRange, node taints, and node memory pressure. A common synthetic pattern is batch window concurrency consuming namespace quota before the reconciliation job starts. Safe next checks are list events, get quota, get pod yaml, and compare requested resources with available capacity.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic RCA Runbook
heading: ImagePullBackOff from internal registry or pull secret
text: For ImagePullBackOff, use pod events to distinguish missing image tag, registry connectivity, pull secret authorization, and service account imagePullSecrets. In the synthetic KOMSCO profile, regulated namespaces use pinned image tags and restricted pull secrets. Safe checks are event messages, image reference, service account imagePullSecrets, and registry operator status. Do not change image tags automatically.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic RCA Runbook
heading: Readiness or liveness probe failure from dependency timeout
text: If readiness or liveness probes fail while the pod is otherwise running, correlate events with previous logs, service endpoints, route latency, and dependency health. For issuance-api, oracle-svc and redis-cache timeouts can cause readiness failure without immediate application crash. Safe checks are pod events, previous logs, endpoints, service DNS, and dependency namespace events.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic RCA Runbook
heading: Route 5xx and backend pod correlation
text: For route 5xx, correlate router metrics, route events, service endpoints, backend pod readiness, and recent deployment changes. If backend endpoints are empty or readiness probes fail, treat application health as the first RCA branch. If router errors rise while endpoints are healthy, inspect ingress controller events and certificate status.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Network Runbook
heading: DNS and NetworkPolicy connectivity triage
section_path_label: Network > DNS or NetworkPolicy > Service connectivity
text: When logs show DNS resolution failure, connection refused, or timeout between namespaces, check service endpoints, DNS operator condition, NetworkPolicy selectors, namespace labels, and route or ingress events. Keep CAS read-only: identify the policy or endpoint mismatch and provide a console link, but do not generate a patch without approval.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Storage Runbook
heading: PVC mount, attach, and detach triage
section_path_label: Storage > PVC > Mount or attach failure
text: For PVC mount failure, inspect PVC phase, bound PV, storage class, pod volume mount events, attach-detach controller warnings, and node availability. If evidence is unavailable due to RBAC, CAS must show missing evidence instead of inventing the storage cause.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Cluster Runbook
heading: ClusterVersion and ClusterOperator condition review
text: Summarize desired version and Available, Progressing, and Degraded conditions. If Progressing or Degraded is true, inspect related ClusterOperator conditions and recent cluster events before judging update risk. For CRC simulations, a healthy ClusterVersion means application-level RCA should remain the primary branch.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Cluster Runbook
heading: Route certificate expiration review
text: For TLS or route failures, inspect route TLS configuration, certificate secret expiration, ingress controller warnings, and recent secret rotation history. CAS should not print certificate private key material or full secret data. Report only metadata, expiry, issuer, and affected route names.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Metric Interpretation Guide
heading: Memory working set versus limit interpretation
section_path_label: Metrics > Pod memory > Working set and limit
text: If container_memory_working_set_bytes is close to or above kube_pod_container_resource_limits for the same pod, and the pod has OOMKilled or restart increase evidence, memory pressure is a strong RCA candidate. If metrics return no-series, do not treat it as healthy; show metric evidence as no-series and continue with events and logs.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Metric Interpretation Guide
heading: Restart increase interpretation
text: A restart increase in the last 30 minutes should be correlated with previous logs and warning events. One restart with no warning event may be low risk, but repeated restart increase with CrashLoopBackOff, OOMKilled, or probe failure should be treated as active RCA work.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Metric Interpretation Guide
heading: Metric no-series handling
text: Metric no-series is not a success signal. CAS should record it as metric evidence and continue with OpenShift events, pod status, and runbook context. If all metric queries fail due to RBAC or NetworkPolicy, show missing evidence with the adapter reason.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Evidence Handling Guide
heading: Minimum evidence pack for regulated RCA
text: A minimum regulated RCA pack contains the user question, target resource, pod status, warning events, previous logs when available, metric summaries, runbook hits, missing evidence reasons, read-only guardrail status, and final answer provider. CAS should preserve evidence IDs and timestamps for later audit storage.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Security Handling Guide
heading: Sensitive data redaction rules
text: CAS must not expose bearer tokens, passwords, secret data, private keys, resident identifiers, account numbers, or raw audit payloads. Logs may be summarized with sensitive values masked. When a user asks for sensitive data, answer with the safe metadata path and explain that the value itself is intentionally redacted.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Batch Operations Guide
heading: Batch window capacity policy
text: During nightly settlement, batch concurrency is capped to protect customer-facing issuance-api. If settlement-worker is Pending, first verify whether the batch window capacity policy intentionally limited concurrency. CAS should distinguish policy-driven Pending from actual cluster capacity shortage.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic SLO Guide
heading: Service health threshold guide
text: Synthetic thresholds. Treat sustained route 5xx over 2 percent for issuance-api as SEV2 candidate, restart increase above three in 30 minutes as active workload risk, and Pending settlement-worker near batch deadline as business-impact risk. Use these as triage hints, not automatic incident declarations.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Operations Knowledge Pack
heading: Customer portal service profile
text: Synthetic customer profile. customer-portal runs in komsco-prod and depends on OIDC metadata, the OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET Secret reference, public route certificates, and issuance-api backend health. After deployment, CrashLoopBackOff often means missing or mismatched Secret/ConfigMap references rather than node failure. CAS must never print secret values; it should report only metadata, reference names, and safe read-only checks.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Known Incident Library
heading: Known incident: customer-portal missing OIDC secret reference
text: Synthetic known incident. A customer-portal rollout may enter CrashLoopBackOff when the Deployment references an OIDC secret key that is absent in the target namespace. Evidence normally includes BackOff events, restart increase, previous logs mentioning a missing OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET or invalid profile, and no memory pressure. Safe next checks are Deployment env references, Secret metadata existence, and rollout history. Do not expose Secret data.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Known Incident Library
heading: Known incident: route 5xx caused by backend readiness loss
text: Synthetic known incident. Route 5xx spikes for issuance-api are often caused by backend readiness failures and endpoint removal, not by the router itself. CAS should correlate route symptoms with pod readiness events, service endpoint availability, previous logs, and SLO thresholds before recommending ingress-controller investigation.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Known Incident Library
heading: Known incident: audit-log-writer PVC attach delay
text: Synthetic known incident. audit-log-writer may remain Pending or ContainerCreating when the audit-evidence PVC attach operation times out. Because this workload protects regulated evidence, CAS should elevate the risk, collect FailedMount events, PVC/PV metadata, StorageClass, node placement, and missing evidence reasons, then hand off storage mutation to the approved change process.

title: KOMSCO Synthetic Known Incident Library
heading: Known incident: dependency timeout from DNS or NetworkPolicy path
section_path_label: Known Incidents > issuance-api > DNS or NetworkPolicy Timeout
text: Synthetic known incident. issuance-api dependency timeouts to redis-cache should be triaged through service endpoints, DNS operator condition, namespace labels, NetworkPolicy selectors, and previous logs. If logs include credentials or connection strings, CAS must summarize with values redacted. Any policy change is approval-required and outside v0.1.1 read-only simulation.
